Event / དུས་ཚིག.1: Tibetan New Year (Losar)
English:
Om! To the deities of the wondrous and auspicious realms,
I prostrate with devotion; please hold me with your compassion.
May the two benefits be spontaneously accomplished, and marvelous auspiciousness arise.
May we be enriched by the glory of virtue, excellence, benefit, and bliss.
May the unchanging Tree of Enlightenment always flourish.
May all thoughts in accordance with the Dharma be fulfilled!
Samaya. This was transcribed onto miraculous paper in the small script of [Yeshe] Tsogyal.
May it meet with the destined emanation of the Dro-mi (nomad) through auspicious coincidence.
This is from the profound Dharma of Sangdak Dorje Dradul (also known as Mingyur Dorje), the heart-son of Garwang Dudjom Lingpa.
Tibetan:
ༀ༔ ངོ་མཚར་བཀྲ་ཤིས་ཞིང་གི་ལྷ་རྣམས་ལ། །གུས་པས་ཕྱག་འཚལ་བརྩེབས་བས་རྗེ་བཟུང་སྟེ། །དོན་གཉིས་ལྷུན་གྲུབ་བཀྲ་ཤིས་རྨད་བྱུང་ཞིང༌། །དགེ་ལེགས་ཕན་བདེའི་དཔལ་གྱིས་ཕུན་སུམ་ཚོགས། །རྟག་ཏུ་མི་འགྱུར་བྱང་ཆུབ་ལྗོན་ཤིང་རྒྱས། །ཆོས་མཐུན་བསམ་པ་མ་ལུས་འགྲུབ་པར་ཤོག

 ས་མ་ཡ༔ མཚོ་རྒྱལ་ཡི་གེ་ཆུང་གཟུངས་སུ༔ འཕྲུལ་གྱི་ཤོག་བུར་ཕབ་ནས་ནི༔ ལས་ཅན་འབྲོག་མིའི་སྤྲུལ་པ་ལ༔ རྟེན་འབྲེལ་དང་བཅས་འཕྲད་པར་ཤོག༔ འདི་ནི་གར་དབང་བདུད་འཇོམས་གླིང་པའི་ཐུགས་སྲས་གསང་བདག་རྡོ་རྗེ་དགྲ་འདུལ་ལམ་མི་འགྱུར་རྡོ་རྗེའི་ཟབ་ཆོས་ལས་བྱུང་བའོ།

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1: Day One: Lama Losar (New Year of the Guru)
English:
The first day is the most significant day, representing faith and auspiciousness.
* Waking up early: People rise before dawn, dress in their finest traditional clothes (Chuba), and perform incense offerings (Sang-sol) to the deities.
* Fetching the First Water: Very early in the morning, the "first water" (Chu-phud) is fetched from a spring or well to be placed on the altar as an offering.
* Making Offerings and Paying Respects: Families gather to enjoy traditional foods like Tsampa and Droma Dresi (sweet buttered rice with small tubers). Afterward, they visit monasteries and temples to pray and make offerings.
* Family Gathering: According to tradition, the first day is reserved for immediate family. People generally stay home together rather than visiting relatives or friends on this day.
Preparations Before the New Year
Before Losar begins, the following items must be prepared:
* Chemar and Droso Chemar: Preparing the auspicious "five-grain bucket" (Chemar) filled with roasted barley flour, butter, and seeds, decorated with colorful butter sculptures.
* Making Khapse: Frying various shapes of traditional Tibetan deep-fried pastries (Khapse).
* House Cleaning: Thoroughly cleaning the entire house to sweep away the bad luck of the previous year.
* New Clothes: Making or buying new clothes for the whole family to wear during the celebrations.
Tibetan:
ཚེས་པ་གཅིག་ནི་དད་པ་དང་བཀྲ་ཤིས་པའི་མཚོན་དོན་ཆེ་ཤོས་ཀྱི་ཉིན་མོ་དེ་ཡིན།
* སྔ་མོ་ལངས་པ། ནམ་མ་ལངས་གོང་ནས་ལངས་ཏེ་གཟབ་མཆོར་སྤྲས་ནས་ལྷ་གསོལ་བསང་གཏོང་བྱེད།
* ཆུ་ཕུད་ལེན་པ། ལོ་གསར་གྱི་ཞོགས་པ་སྔ་པོ་ནས་ཆུ་མིག་ནས་ཆུ་ཕུད་བླངས་ཏེ་མཆོད་བཤམ་བྱེད།
* མཆོད་མཇལ་ཞུ་བ། ནང་མི་ལྷན་འཛོམས་ཀྱིས་རྩམ་པ་དང་གྲོ་མ་འབྲས་བསིལ་སོགས་ཀྱི་ཞལ་ཟས་ལ་རོལ། དེ་ནས་དགོན་པ་དང་ལྷ་ཁང་དུ་མཆོད་མཇལ་ལ་འགྲོ།
* ནང་མི་མཉམ་འཛོམས། སྲོལ་རྒྱུན་གྱི་ལྟ་སྟངས་ལྟར་ན། ཚེས་གཅིག་ཉིན་ནང་མི་མཉམ་དུ་བཞུགས་པ་ལས་ཕྱི་ལ་གཉེན་འཕྲད་སོགས་ལ་འགྲོ་སྲོལ་དེ་ཙམ་མེད།
ལོ་གསར་མ་ཚེས་གོང་ནས་གཤམ་གསལ་རྣམས་གྲ་སྒྲིག་བྱེད་དགོས།
* ཕྱེ་མར་དང་གྲོ་སོ་ཕྱེ་མར།
* ཁ་ཟས་བཟོ་བ།
* ཁང་པ་གད་བདར་བྱེད་པ།
* དུག་ལོག་གསར་པ་བཟོ་བ།

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1: Day Two: Gyalpo Losar (King's New Year)
English:
This day is dedicated to visiting and exchanging greetings with relatives and friends.
* Visiting Relatives: People start going out to visit the homes of relatives, friends, and teachers to celebrate the New Year together.
* Tashi Delek (Auspicious Greetings): People offer each other ceremonial scarves (Khata), present the auspicious Chemar (barley flour and butter offering), and offer traditional barley beer (Chang) as a welcome.
* Enjoying Feasts: Large meals are hosted where guests enjoy a variety of Tibetan dishes, particularly traditional Tibetan butter tea and barley beer, in a joyful atmosphere.
Tibetan:
ཉིན་འདི་ནི་གཉེན་འབྲེལ་དང་མཛའ་གྲོགས་བར་འཚམས་འདྲི་བྱེད་པའི་ཉིན་མོ་དེ་ཡིན།
* གཉེན་འཕྲད་དུ་འགྲོ་བ། གཉེན་ཉེ་དང་། གྲོགས་པོ། དགེ་རྒན་སོགས་ཀྱི་ནང་དུ་ལོ་གསར་ལ་འགྲོ་བའི་འགོ་འཛུགས་ཀྱི་རེད།
* བཀྲ་ཤིས་བདེ་ལེགས། ཕན་ཚུན་ལ་ཁ་བཏགས་སྒྲོན་པ་དང་། གྲོ་སོ་ཕྱེ་མར་འདེགས་པ། ཆང་སྐྱེམས་འདྲེན་པ་སོགས་བྱེད།
* གསོལ་སྟོན་ལ་རོལ་བ། མགྲོན་པོ་བཤམས་ནས་བོད་ཀྱི་ཟས་རིགས་སྣ་ཚོགས་དང་ལྷག་པར་དུ་བོད་ཇ་དང་ཆང་ལ་རོལ་ནས་སྐྱིད་པོ་བྱེད།

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1: Day Three: Chos-Kyong Losar (New Year of the Protectors)
English:
This is the day dedicated to making offerings to the Dharma Protectors and accumulating merit.
* Changing Prayer Flags: People hoist new prayer flags (Dar-chog) on their rooftops and mountain tops. They also perform elaborate incense burning ceremonies (Sang-sol).
* Chanting "Lha-Gyal-Lo": Large groups of people gather to shout "Kye Kye Lha Gyal Lo!" (Victory to the Deities!), throwing roasted barley flour into the air as an auspicious prayer for success and protection.
* Festivities and Entertainment: From this day onward, public celebrations increase with traditional folk songs, dances, and various forms of community entertainment.

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1: Incense Offering (Sangsol) at the local Tenkhar
English:
The Source of Happiness and Fortune
A Concise Smoke Offering to Gesar
by Sera Khandro
 
om ah hung
oṃ āḥ hūṃ
zakmé yeshe dütsi sang chö di
I present this smoke offering of pure wisdom nectar
rik sum sempé gyutrul sengchen gyal
To the Great Lion, King Gesar, magical emanation of the bodhisattvas of the Three Families,
kakhor lejé sungmé tsok la bul
And to the assembly of servants and guardians.
zhé la sampé dön kün drubpar dzö
Please accept it and accomplish all our wishes.
 
Thus, prompted by laziness, this concise text was written by the one named Dewé Dorje during the accumulation of a specific number of recitations.
Tibetan:
གེ་སར་བསང་བསྡུས་བདེ་ལེགས་འབྱུང་གནས་བཞུགས༔
 
ཨོཾ་ཨཱཿཧཱུྃ༔
ཟག་མེད་ཡེ་ཤེས་བདུད་རྩིའི་བསང་མཆོད་འདི༔
རིག་གསུམ་སེམས་དཔའི་སྒྱུ་འཕྲུལ་སེང་ཆེན་རྒྱལ༔
བཀའ་འཁོར་ལས་བྱེད་སྲུང་མའི་ཚོགས་ལ་འབུལ༔
བཞེས་ལ་བསམ་པའི་དོན་ཀུན་འགྲུབ་པར་མཛོད༔
 ཅེས་བདེ་བའི་རྡོ་རྗེའི་མིང་གིས་གྲངས་ཚད་གསོག་སྐབས་ལེ་ལོས་བསྐུལ་ནས་ཉུང་བསྡུས་འདི་བྲིས་པའོ། །
གེ་སར་བསང་བསྡུས་བདེ་ལེགས་འབྱུང་གནས་བཞུགས༔

English:
The one and only father of all Buddhas is Mañjuśrī,

To you I pray! Bless my mind and inspire my understanding!
Texts by and about Kyabje Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche, Tashi Paljor (dil mgo mkhyen brtse bkra shis dpal 'byor, 1910-1991):

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1: Event
Tsechu Offering for the Month of the Monkey
The Day of the Universal Incense Offering (Dzamling Spyi Sang)
English:
In the 8th century, after the completion of the Samye Mi-gyur Lhun-gyi Drupa temple (Samye Monastery), Guru Padmasambhava subdued the fierce spirits and deities of Tibet. This day commemorates the occasion when he performed an incense offering (Sang) to all the local deities and guardians of the entire world.
The Purpose: It is observed for the sake of world peace, to create harmony between gods, spirits, and humans, and to clear away all obstacles.

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1: Birthday of His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama
English:
Gang-ri ra-wä kor-wä zhing-kham dir
In the land encircled by a wall of snow mountains,
Phän-dang de-wa ma-lü jung-wä nä
The source of all happiness and benefit without exception,
Chän-rä-zig-wang Tän-dzin Gya-tsho la
Is the Lord of Compassion, Tenzin Gyatso.
Zhab-pä si-thä bar-du tän-gyur chig
May your lotus feet remain steadfast until the end of existence.

༸སྐྱབས་རྗེ་བདུད་འཇོམས་ཡང་སྲིད་བསྟན་འཛིན་ཡེ་ཤེས་རྡོ་རྗེ་རིན་པོ་ཆེའི་
འཁྲུངས་སྐར།
Birthday of His Holiness Dudjom Yangsi, Tenzin Yeshe Dorje Rinpoche.
English:
Brief Long-Life Prayer for Kyabje Dudjom Yangsi Tenzin Yeshi Dorjee Rinpoche.
Rig-kün khyab-dag dö-mä gön-po dang
To the Primordial Protector, the All-pervading Lord of all Buddha families,
Yer-me Tän-dzin Ye-she Dor-je chog
Inseparable from the supreme Tenzin Yeshe Dorje,
Sang-sum dor-je'i tri la zhab-tän nä
May your secret Three Vajras (Body, Speech, and Mind) remain firm upon the Vajra Throne,
Thrin-lä nam-khä tha la khyab gyur chig
And may your enlightened activities pervade the furthest reaches of space!

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1:
༸སྐྱབས་རྗེ་ཀ་ཐོག་དགེ་བརྩེ་རིན་པོ་ཆེའི་འདས་མཆོད། 
Parinirvana of H.H. kathok gertse  Rinpoche
English:
Supplication to the 4th Katok Getse Rinpoche
Master of the entire Doctrine of the Buddha, the Katokpa,
Upholder of the Victory Banner of the Teachings with Unchanging Activity,
Crown Ornament of millions of scholars and realized masters, the Getsepa,
To the Fearless Lord of Speech, I pray!
This beautiful supplication (gsol 'debs) for the 4th H.H. Katok Getse Rinpoche, Gyurme Tenpa Gyaltsen, was composed by the H.H. Kyabje Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche

English:
Long Life Prayer for the Supreme Katok Situ Rinpoche

OM SWASTI | NGE DÖN CHÖ KYI DRÖN ME TONG BAR WAY
Om Swasti! Through the thousand-fold radiance of the Lamp of Definitive Dharma,
 ZHING DIR SHAY DRUB NANG WAY GO GYA JAY
You open a hundred doors of teaching and practice within this realm.
TRIN LAY KÜN TU KHYAB DAK NIR MA KA
All-pervading Lord of enlightened activities, Nirmaka (Emanation),
PAL DEN ZANG PO ZHAB PAY TAK TEN SOL
Glorious and Excellent One, may your lotus feet remain forever firm

Long Life Prayer (Zhabten) for the current incarnation of H.E.Katok Situ Rinpoche, composed by the late Great Master Chatral Sangye Dorje Rinpoche

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1:
༸སྐྱབས་རྗེ་གདུང་སྲས་རྣམ་རྒྱལ་ཟླ་བ་རིན་པོ་ཆེའི་ཁྲུངས་སྐར།
Birthday of Kyabje Namgye Dawa Rinpoche
English:
Brief Long-Life Prayer for Kyabje Dudjom Yangsi Tenzin Yeshi Dorjee Rinpoche.
Rig-kün khyab-dag dö-mä gön-po dang
To the Primordial Protector, the All-pervading Lord of all Buddha families,

Yer-me Tän-dzin Ye-she Dor-je chog
Inseparable from the supreme Tenzin Yeshe Dorje,

Sang-sum dor-je'i tri la zhab-tän nä
May your secret Three Vajras (Body, Speech, and Mind) remain firm upon the Vajra Throne,

Thrin-lä nam-khä tha la khyab gyur chig
And may your enlightened activities pervade the furthest reaches of space!

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1: Event

༸གོང་ས་མཆོག་ལ་ཞི་བདེའི་གཟེངས་རྟགས་ཕུལ་བའི་དུས་དྲན།
Anniversary of the Nobel Peace Prize: Commemorating the award presented to His Holiness the Dalai Lama.
English:
Gang-ri ra-wä kor-wä zhing-kham dir
In the land encircled by a wall of snow mountains,

Phän-dang de-wa ma-lü jung-wä nä
The source of all happiness and benefit without exception,

Chän-rä-zig-wang Tän-dzin Gya-tsho la
Is the Lord of Compassion, Tenzin Gyatso.

Zhab-pä si-thä bar-du tän-gyur chig
May your lotus feet remain steadfast until the end of existence.

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1: Parinirvana of Kyabje Dungse Thinley Norbu Rinpoche (Dudjom Tersar, 1931–2011).
Parinirvana of Trakthung Dudjom Lingpa (Dudjom Tersar lineage).
Parinirvana of Kyabje Taklung Tsetrul Rinpoche (Dorje Drak, 1926–2015).
Maha Parinirvana Puja: Ceremonies and practice related to the memorial of Kyabje Dudjom Jikdrel Yeshe Dorje Rinpoche (1904–1987).
Parinirvana of Khenchen Jigme Phuntsok (Palyul lineage, 1933–2004).
Annual Monastic Holiday: Commencement of the monks' annual vacation.
Parinirvana of Chatral Sangye Dorje Rinpoche (Kathok lineage, 1913–2015).
Birthday of the 4th Kyabje Drubwang Rinpoche (Palyul lineage).
Parinirvana of Kyabje Dodrupchen Rinpoche (1927–2022).

༸སྐྱབས་རྗེ་གདུང་སྲས་འཕྲིན་ལས་ནོར་བུའི་ཡང་སྲིད་ཛྙཱ་ནཱ་ཏ་རིན་པོ་ཆེའི་འཁྲུངས་སྐར།
Birthday. of Kyabje Yangshi Dungse Gyana ta Rinpoche
English:
Long Life Prayer for the Dungse Gyana Ta Reincarnation of Kyabje Dungse Thinley Norbu Rinpoche

OM SWASTI | TSE LHA RIG DZIN GYA TSOY NGO WO NYID 
Om Swasti! You are the very essence of the ocean of Longevity Deities and Vidyadharas.
CHI ME TSO KYE DOR JE JIN LAP KYI 
Through the blessings of the Deathless Lake-Born Vajra (Guru Rinpoche),
DUNG SE JNA NA KAL GYAR ZHAB TEN CHING May the lotus feet of Dungse Jnana remain firm for hundreds of eons,
LAP CHEN TEN DROY DZAY PA THAR CHIN SHOK 
And may your vast enlightened activities for the teachings and beings be perfectly fulfilled!
~ At the persistent request of Lama Ösel Dorje Namgyal Wangdu, this prayer was written by one who bears the name of the Dudjom Tulku, Sangye Pema Zhepa. It was composed on the 10th day of the Tibetan month in the Wood-Horse year of the 17th Rabjung cycle. May these aspirations be meaningfully realized

Event / དུས་ཚིག.1: Event
Nyingma Monlam Chenmo: Commencement of the Great Nyingma Prayer Festival in Bodh Gaya.
Gutor Preparation: Training for the Gutor rituals, including ritual cake construction and Practice Vajara dances (Cham).
Parinirvana of Kyabje Dudjom Sangye Pema Zhepa Rinpoche (1990–2022).
Parinirvana of Gyalwa Longchen Rabjam (1308–1364).
Parinirvana of Rabjam Gyurme Chokyi Senge Rinpoche.
Return Date: Monks return to the monastery from their holiday.
Gutor Commencement: Year-end wrathful rituals and repelling ceremonies.
